name: tabula-mcp
description: Let Claude work with people in shared Tabula Markdown workspaces.
long_description: Turn Markdown created in Claude or local files into shared Tabula workspaces. Claude can start or join encrypted live sessions, work with Markdown files and comment threads, export fixed encrypted copies, and import a #json copy for local materialization. Room and copy keys stay in URL fragments; relays and snapshot storage receive encrypted data.

tools.name: start_session
tools.description: Use this when, and only when, the user explicitly wants a new live Tabula.md session or encrypted Markdown collaboration handoff. Never use it for Codex or Claude artifacts, private drafts, fixed Copies, or existing #room URLs.

tools.name: join_room
tools.description: Use this when the user provides a private Tabula.md #room URL. Join that existing live session, keep the URL private, and do not start a replacement session.

tools.name: leave_session
tools.description: Use this when the user asks the agent to disconnect from one live Tabula.md session. It does not delete the Room or its files.

tools.name: list_files
tools.description: Use this when the target path is unknown or a directory view is needed inside a connected Tabula.md session. Do not use it for the host's local filesystem.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Use this when reading one Markdown file or bounded line range inside a connected Tabula.md session. Do not use it for local or host-native files.

tools.name: read_multiple_files
tools.description: Use this when reading a known small batch of complete Markdown files with revisions inside a connected Tabula.md session. Do not use it for local files.

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Use this when searching paths or Markdown content with nearby line context inside a connected Tabula.md session. Do not use it for the host's local filesystem.

tools.name: list_comments
tools.description: Use this when finding open, resolved, or all product comment threads inside a connected Tabula.md session before acting on a comment ID.

tools.name: add_comment
tools.description: Use this when the user wants a file-level or inclusive line-anchored product comment added inside a connected Tabula.md session.

tools.name: reply_to_comment
tools.description: Use this when replying to an existing product comment thread inside a connected Tabula.md session. Obtain its comment ID from List Comments.

tools.name: resolve_comment
tools.description: Use this when resolving or reopening an existing product comment thread inside a connected Tabula.md session.

tools.name: delete_comment
tools.description: Use this when the user explicitly wants to permanently delete an existing product comment thread and its replies from a connected Tabula.md session.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Use this when creating or replacing one complete Markdown file inside a connected Tabula.md session. Read an existing file first and pass its revision; do not use it for local files.

tools.name: write_files
tools.description: Use this when one atomic multi-file create or replace operation is needed inside a connected Tabula.md session. Pass revisions for existing files; do not use it for local files.

tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Use this when making small exact-text replacements inside one Markdown file in a connected Tabula.md session. Read it first and pass its revision; stale edits rebase only on safe matches.

tools.name: create_directory
tools.description: Use this when creating a directory and missing parents inside a connected Tabula.md session. An existing directory is a no-op; this does not affect local folders.

tools.name: move_file
tools.description: Use this when moving or renaming one file or directory inside a connected Tabula.md session. The destination parent must exist, and files require their current revision.

tools.name: delete_path
tools.description: Use this when the user explicitly wants to delete a path inside a connected Tabula.md session. Files need revisions and non-empty directories require recursive true.

tools.name: import_copy
tools.description: Use this when the user provides a private Tabula.md #json URL. It returns relative Markdown files but does not join a live Room or write them to the local filesystem.

tools.name: export_copy
tools.description: Use this when the user wants a fixed encrypted Tabula.md #json handoff from files or a live session. Use Start Session instead for continued live collaboration.
